name: stop overthinking
desc: We’ve all been there: the endless loops of I should have said... or What if they think... Overthinking feels like a heavy fog that keeps us from seeing the beauty of the present. Stop Overthinking is more than just a self-help book; it is a gentle invitation to let go of the need for absolute certainty. Through relatable examples and science-backed psychology, it teaches us that peace isn't the absence of thoughts—it’s the ability to choose which ones we listen to.If you’re tired of feeling exhausted by your own mind, this book is the deep breath you’ve been waiting for

name: How to Finish What You Start
desc:
How many times have you started a project with great enthusiasm, only to find it abandoned in the corner of forgetfulness? The gap between starting and finishing is where our most important dreams and goals get lost.

The book How to Finish What You Start is your practical guide to closing that gap. It dives into the psychology of follow-through and achievement, analyzing the hidden reasons behind laziness and loss of passion, while providing concrete strategies to build unshakable self-discipline.

If you are tired of unfinished projects and want to turn your ideas into reality, this book is the engine you need to reach the finish line

name: Rich Dad Poor Dad
desc:
Did you ever learn in school how to make money work for you, instead of you working for money? The book Rich Dad Poor Dad shatters the inherited myths about job security and a monthly salary.

Through the author's real-life story of two fathers with contrasting views on money, Robert Kiyosaki highlights the fundamental difference between 'Assets' that put money in your pocket and Liabilities that take it out. This book is the first step toward changing your financial mindset and achieving the freedom many dream of.

What you will learn in this book:

    Why academic education alone doesn't guarantee wealth.

    How to distinguish between true investments and disguised expenses.

    The most important lesson: The rich don't work for money; they make money work for them

name: The Psychology of Money
desc:
Do you think financial success depends only on intelligence and mathematical calculations? The book Psychology of Money proves the exact opposite. Author Morgan Housel explains that managing money is more about your behavior and dealing with emotions than your knowledge of complex equations.

    Through short stories and deep lessons you learn that true wealth is what others do not see and how patience and discipline are the main engines for building sustainable wealth. This book completely changes your perspective on saving and investing and makes you realize that owning money gives you the most important thing in life which is the freedom to control your time.

    If you want to understand the psychological reasons behind your wrong financial decisions and how to build a healthy relationship with money then this book is your comprehensive and practical guide
